body.u: Looking back over my life I feel that my job was important for security knowing that I have had a fairly good job and family life. My job was very important to me and as a result of my secretererialsecretarial studies I have had a fairly comfortable job with good promotion prospects leaving me in a fairly high position in a large office. I did not want to have anything really fantastic just a reasonably job with worth while meaning in my life. With the amount of money that I need to help support a family, to live as I was acustomedaccustomed when living with my parents. Not to have to struggle to make ends meet. A family was also very important with an average home and enough money to give to my children a good education and a happy life.
My ideal family would be two children one boy and one girl. A husband who has security in a job.
Looking back at my 50 years after leaving school, I found that any major decision before marriage was made myself with very little influence on decisions of jobs.
I always needed to be involved in sport if not taking part spectating. A good social life not being stuck at home evening after evening but getting out.
Seeing different countries and places in England seeing how other the other people live in other countries. Meeting people and having many interests to fall back on when retiring.
